Stateline Guide

Tri-County Regional Marketing Guide

For new and existing businesses, artists, nonprofits, galleries, programs, services, and mentorships trying to expand customers, visibility, partnerships, and usefulness across Colfax, Las Animas, and Huerfano counties.

Plan your growth Find the network Understand the region

Mobile quick route

Start here

Use this guide to find the right directory, funding source, event channel, arts route, or update path.

Search all listingsFundingArts & CultureSubmit update

Purpose of the guide

Use this guide when you need a route, not just a search result.

This guide does not replace chambers, tourism sites, newspapers, directories, calendars, or public offices. It helps people use those channels in the right order, for the right purpose, and with a clear next step.

Use it when you have a business, event, nonprofit, gallery, class, service, or program and need to know where to put it so people across Colfax, Las Animas, and Huerfano counties can find it.

01

Plan Your Growth

Choose what kind of expansion you need before chasing every possible listing.

Start the growth cycle 02

Find the Network

Use existing directories, calendars, media channels, chambers, and support orgs first.

Search shortcuts and leads 03

Understand the Region

See how Colfax, Las Animas, Huerfano, and regional channels fit together.

Read the regional map

Quick task guides

Start with the search question people actually type.

These pages turn common local questions into next actions: where to post, where to advertise, what county page to use, and which channels to verify first.

Where to post events in Raton, New Mexico

Find starting points for Raton event visibility, including city pages, tourism routes, media, calendars, public boards, partners, and verification steps.

Where to post events in Trinidad, Colorado

Use Trinidad tourism, city, chamber, creative district, venue, media, and community routes to submit or promote public events after verifying rules.

Where to post events in Walsenburg and La Veta

Find Huerfano County event routes through Spanish Peaks Country, Walsenburg, La Veta, libraries, media, arts, and tourism channels.

Where to advertise or promote something in Trinidad, Colorado

Compare Trinidad-area promotion routes, including tourism channels, chamber options, media, venue lineups, newsletters, and paid-placement inquiries.

Colfax County business resources

Start with Raton business services, licensing, GrowRaton, MainStreet, county resources, tourism, and New Mexico support before outreach.

Las Animas County nonprofit resources

Find Trinidad and Las Animas nonprofit visibility, grant, partner, media, chamber, and community-resource routes with verification reminders.

Huerfano County event calendars and visitor listings

Use Spanish Peaks Country, Walsenburg, La Veta, media, tourism, library, arts, and community calendars as Huerfano event starting points.

Artist and gallery promotion across the tri-county area

Route art shows, gallery events, makers, workshops, performances, and creative-sector announcements through arts, tourism, media, venue, and partner channels.

Regional newsletters, event calendars, and visitor guides

Compare tri-county newsletters, event calendars, tourism guides, business directories, venue lineups, and placement inquiry paths.

Pick your door

Use the guide by need, not by reading order.

Open a gallery, fix an old listing, plan a fundraiser, or expand a rural service from the page that fits the task. The same useful loop still applies: list, post, partner, follow up, repeat.

Find amplifier channels

Routing logic

The same loop works across roles.

New businesses, long-running organizations, artists, mentors, venues, and service providers can start from different pages because the guide is organized around tasks: getting listed, posting something, finding partners, preparing materials, and verifying the path.

Use by role

Six quick starts for the people this guide is meant to serve.

New business

Pick one target customer and get one official listing or support conversation started.

Plan, Network, County page, Appendix

Existing business

Update listings, add one cross-county channel, and track whether it produces calls, visits, or referrals.

Network, Amplifiers, Templates

Nonprofit or program

Write the community benefit in one sentence and choose calendars, partners, and referral sources before ads.

Plan, Posting, Templates, Appendix

Artist, gallery, or maker

Pair the creative offer with a place and date, then route it through arts, tourism, and venue channels.

Amplifiers, Region, County page

Event organizer

Prepare the packet once, then submit to the right calendar, venue, tourism page, and media outlet.

Amplifiers, Templates, Posting

Rural service or mentor

Make referral language easy: who you help, what happens next, and how a partner sends someone to you.

Plan, Network, Appendix

County color + landmark cues

Three counties should feel related, not identical.

Use these as visual and editorial anchors for ads, event pages, business spotlights, and nonprofit campaigns.

Colfax

Raton Pass and the Santa Fe Trail corridor. Use green route cues for mountain, pass, and northern New Mexico campaigns.

Las Animas

Trinidad History Museum and the Corazon de Trinidad district. Use clay red cues for downtown, arts, and coalfield heritage.

Huerfano

Francisco Fort Museum and the Spanish Peaks corridor. Use blue-violet cues for valley, peaks, and southern Colorado routes.

Inventory backbone

632 local listings and leads, organized beside existing directory shortcuts.

632local listings and leads
236Huerfano
176Colfax
122Las Animas
98Regional

Use the local inventory for outreach and discovery. Details can change, so open the page when available and submit a correction when a listing, link, or contact route is outdated.

Download CSV Download JSON Download Sources

Existing shortcuts

Start with places that already collect the information.

Try keywords like funding, calendar, artist, nonprofit, Raton, Trinidad, Walsenburg, chamber, or grants.

Colfax Member business directory

Angel Fire Chamber Member Business Directory

Angel Fire and Moreno Valley businesses, nonprofits, artists, lodging, dining, services, and chamber member categories.

Use as a broader Angel Fire directory before searching by category or building a manual list.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Regional Artist directory

Artists Sunday Artist Directory

Artists and makers seeking a broader public artist directory, including existing La Veta-area examples.

Use as a supplemental discovery and listing path for regional artists; verify current signup rules before recommending.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Regional Arts / civic grants

Arts in Society Grant

Colorado artists, arts organizations, schools, governments, and cross-sector projects using art to address civic or social challenges.

Use for Las Animas and Huerfano arts/community projects that have a clear public benefit and partnership frame.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Huerfano Community calendar

Bachman Community Calendar

Farmers markets and recurring local event timing across La Veta, Cuchara, Gardner, and Walsenburg.

Use to avoid calendar conflicts and find offline outreach opportunities.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Las Animas Local nonprofit grants

Bar NI Community Fund

Purgatoire Valley 501(c)(3) nonprofits and service organizations seeking project support in youth development, education, environment, health, human services, civic work, and public benefit.

Use for Trinidad-area nonprofit projects, then verify the current spring or fall cycle before promising a deadline.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Las Animas Regional business development

Bent County Business Development

Southeast Colorado business-development contact paths that may support Las Animas-area users.

Use as a regional referral lead and verify coverage before sending users there.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Colfax Licensing / compliance

City of Raton Business Licenses and Regulations

Raton business registration, zoning checks, and local licensing steps.

Use before publishing a startup checklist for Raton-based ventures.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Colfax Municipal business resources

City of Raton Business Services

Official Raton business services, permits, licenses, economic development, and support pages.

Use as the official city business hub; if a direct directory path changes, start here.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Colfax Startup / economic-development resource directory

City of Raton Economic Development Programs

Raton and Colfax startup paths, SBA/SBDC/SCORE/FastTrac links, GrowRaton, Raton MainStreet, and related support routes.

Use when someone needs a first route into business support rather than a single directory listing.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Colfax Business support

City of Raton Local Business Support

Hands-on startup help, SBA, SBDC, GrowRaton, Raton MainStreet, SCORE, and city support paths.

Use when a new venture needs the first phone call or support form.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Las Animas Economic development updates

City of Trinidad Economic Development Newsletter

Trinidad and Las Animas news, resources, and event updates from city economic development.

Use to track city-backed economic development opportunities.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Las Animas Funding

City of Trinidad Grants

Grant notices for nonprofits and community-serving organizations.

Use for local funding checks before searching statewide grant lists.

Details can change. Open the page, then submit a correction if this pathway is outdated.

Open the full shortcut directory

Listing updates

Submit or correct a listing

Use this when a business, nonprofit, gallery, program, service, venue, event series, or resource should be added, updated, corrected, or removed. Submissions are starting points for review, not automatic publication.

What to include

Name, county, community, website or source link, contact route, short description, audience served, and what action a reader should take.

Placeholder intake contact

Email: updates@statelineguide.example
Phone: (575) 000-0000
Mail: 000 Main Street, Raton, NM 87740

Open submission form

Next action

Choose the next practical route.

  • Plan the right outreach cycle before collecting links
  • Search public directories and local leads
  • Find event calendars, newsletters, visitor guides, and directory channels
  • Choose a county starting point

Choose your path

Start from the kind of work you are trying to make happen.

The guide is most useful when a user can identify the job, prepare one packet, choose a channel set, and save proof. These four routes translate the whole site into plain next steps for common users.

I am an artist

Use this route for buyers, commissions, shows, workshops, markets, public art, or collaborations.

  1. Prepare a portfolio packet with images, bio, medium, price range, travel radius, and contact route.
  2. Use arts, venue, gallery, tourism, and calendar routes that match the offer.
  3. Send selective outreach, then track who replied and what they requested.
Open artist and gallery routes

I run a business

Use this route when the goal is customers, referrals, bookings, or a cleaner public listing.

  1. Confirm name, address or service area, hours, license path, contact route, and customer action.
  2. Update existing directories before paying for ads or building new lists.
  3. Test one matched channel and track calls, visits, referrals, or bookings.
Plan a business visibility loop

I organize events

Use this route when a date is set and people need to find the event in time.

  1. Prepare one event packet with title, date, place, image, description, accessibility notes, and call to action.
  2. Submit to calendars, venue lineups, tourism routes, media, and partner channels that fit the audience.
  3. Save listing links, screenshots, shares, attendance notes, and follow-up contacts.
Choose an event posting route

I serve on a nonprofit board

Use this route when outreach must show public benefit, partnership value, and documentation.

  1. Name the public benefit in one sentence before choosing channels.
  2. Use partner, calendar, media, volunteer, and referral routes before paid promotion.
  3. Save proof for board packets, sponsors, grant reports, and future asks.
Use nonprofit-ready templates
Back to top

Immediate directory

Find the right route.

Search by need, county, town, audience, or task. Results include practical links and update reminders so users can act without treating old details as final.

Results update after submitting the form or after a short pause while typing. Press Escape to close this panel.

Open resource directory Submit a correction

Colfax NM + Las Animas CO + Huerfano CO

This guide helps businesses, artists, nonprofits, programs, venues, and service providers find the right regional route faster.

See something outdated? Send a source link through Submit Update so the guide can improve.
